blockingIssues.description: The same-workspace read-isolation helper derives test user IDs from time.Now().UnixNano() modulo arithmetic, which can collide under the mission's shared-schema parallel test topology and makes the security proof nondeterministic.
The original write-isolation feature did not cover the documented restart/continue mutation surface required by VAL-SEC-TRACK-003; it only covered update, stop, delete, and batch patch denial.

The attempted restart/continue fix still does not exercise a real documented continue/restart mutation surface. It treats UpdateTimeEntry with Stop=nil as a stopped-to-running conversion even though the service contract preserves the existing stop and mission guidance says continue should create a new running entry.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Explicitly forbid time-based pseudo-unique identifiers such as time.Now().UnixNano() for shared-schema parallel backend tests, and require deterministic collision-safe test-owned identity helpers instead.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: The failed review for tracking-security-cross-user-read-isolation found time.Now().UnixNano() % 100000000000 in apps/backend/internal/tracking/application/security_regressions_test.go, which undermines repeatable parallel security proof under the shared opentoggl_test schema.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Reinforce that workers must read the required .factory/library architecture and traceability files before implementation, and treat missing prerequisite reads as a process failure instead of optional context.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: The tracking-security-cross-user-write-isolation transcript skeleton showed mission-doc and skill reads but not the required .factory/library/architecture.md and .factory/library/documentation-traceability.md reads.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Emphasize that if a requested regression path cannot be found in the documented or implemented contract, the worker must stop and return to orchestrator instead of inferring semantics through a nearby internal API.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: The failed fix feature tracking-security-cross-user-restart-write-proof-fix inferred restart/continue semantics through UpdateTimeEntry with Stop=nil even though service code and mission guidance define continue/restart differently.

rejectedObservations.observation: Handoffs can contain non-resolvable full commit SHAs and shared-state guidance should be updated immediately.
